original: Junko Tabei from Japan becomes 1st woman to reach summit of Mt Everest
Farmer Sugeng finds 1.2 million year old Pithecanthropus IX skull

NBC broadcasts "Motown 25", a concert celebrating the anniversary of Motown Record held in Pasadena, California in March; performers include: The Miracles; The Temptations; The Four Tops;Diana Rossandthe Supremes; The Jackson 5; andMichael Jackson, who unveils his "moonwalk" dance move
Nancy Roman, American, NASA's first chief of astronomy 'Mother of the Hubble', born in Nashville, Tennessee (d. 2018)
Venera 5 lands on Venus, returns data on atmosphere
Theodore Maiman operates the first optical laser at Hughes Research Laboratories in Malibu, California
Michael Jordanis named NBA Rookie of the Year
The Campbell Soup Company introduces SpaghettiOs under its Franco-American brand
Italian inventor Angelo Moriondo receives patent for 1st espresso machine
Johannes Thingnes Bø, Norwegian biathlete (5 x Olympic gold 2018, 22; 12 x World C'ship gold), born in Stryn, Norway
Philadelphia Phillies slugger Greg Luzinski hits home run into the Liberty Bell monument at Philadelphia's Veteran's Stadium, an estimated 500-foot shot
MLB Montreal Expos trailing San Francisco Giants by 9 runs comeback to win 14-13
Thomas Brodie-Sangster, English actor (Sam in "Love Actually"; Nanny McPhee; The Artful Dodger), born in London
Action film "Top Gun" starringTom CruiseandVal Kilmer, directed by Tony Scott, is released across the US
US space shuttle STS-49 lands (maiden voyage of Endeavour)

Verne Gagne beats Mad Dog Vachon in Omaha, to become NWA champ
Remains of a neanderthal man found on Jersey, Channel Islands
Maria Gaetana Agnesi, Italian mathematician and philosopher, first woman appointed as a mathematics professor at a university, born in Milan, Italy (d. 1799)
Margret Rey, German-American author and illustrator (Curious George), born in Hamburg, Germany (d. 1996)
French heroineJoan of Arcis canonized as a saint by Pope Benedict XV
Hana Brady, Jewish Czech Holocaust victim (Hanna's Suitcase), born in Prague (d. 1944)
Gordon Cooper completes 22 orbits in Faith 7, ends US Project Mercury
Belgian-French engineer Étienne Lenoir builds the first automobile with an internal-combustion engine
Elizabeth Palmer Peabody, American educator, founder of first English-language kindergarten in the US, born in Billerica, Massachusetts (d. 1894)
Joan Benoit Samuelson, American long distance athlete (Olympic gold, marathon 1984; WR 1980, 82; Boston Marathon 1979, 83), born in Cape Elizabeth, Maine
Dafydd "Dave" Williams, Canadian physician and astronaut (sk: STS 90), born in Saskatoon, Saskatchewan
Anja Mittag, German soccer striker (158 caps; Turbine Potsdam, FC Rosengård; Olympic gold 2016), born in Chemnitz, Germany

Corey Perry, Canadian ice hockey left wing  (Stanley Cup 2007 Mighty Ducks of Anaheim; Hart Memorial Trophy 2011; Olympic gold 2010, 14; World C'ship gold 2016 [captain]; NHL All-Star 2008, 11, 12, 16), born in New Liskeard, Ontario
Muhammad Alibeats Alfredo Evangelist in 15 for heavyweight boxing title
Muhammad AliTKOs Ron Lyle in 11 for heavyweight boxing title

Capitol Records releases the Beach Boys 11th studio album "Pet Sounds"; the groundbreaking work includes hit singles "Sloop John B", "Wouldn't It Be Nice", and "God Only Knows"

Joseph Polchinski, American leading theoretical physicist (multiverse), born in White Plains, New York (d. 2018)
Johannes Georg Bednorz, German physicist, winner of 1987 Nobel Prize in Physics for discovering high-temperature superconductivity in ceramics, born in Neuenkirchen, West Germany

Thurman Thomas, American College/Pro Football HOF running back (Oklahoma State Uni; NFL MVP, NFL Offensive Player of the Year 1991; First-team All-Pro 1990, 91; 5 x Pro Bowl; Buffalo Bills), born in Houston, Texas
Stanley Cup Final, Pacific Coliseum, Vancouver, BC: 3-peat for NY Islanders; sweep Vancouver Canucks in 4 games with a 3-1 Game 4 win
Japanese poet Matsuo Bashō leaves Edo on his epic 150-day journey to Honshu Island in Japan, which he writes about in the literary masterpiece "Oku no Hosomichi" (The Narrow Road to the Deep North)
World's first electric tram enters service in Lichterfelde near Berlin
The first regularly scheduled transatlantic flights begin between Idlewild Airport (New York International Airport) and Heathrow Airport (London), operated by El Al Israel Airlines
Denmark abolishes the slave trade
First recorded dam disaster in the US occurs in Williamsburg, Massachusetts
Earthquake kills 47 in Japan
108°F (42°C) in Blitzen, Oregon
1st AL night game, Philadelphia Shribe Park (Indians 8, Athletics 3 in 10)

Mississippi River steamboat service begins
Kraft Theater presents an act from "Profiles in Courage"
